title: 构建能持续运行数小时的智能体：Anthropic 工程师揭秘对抗式生成-评估架构
author: AI Engineer
content:
Build Agents That Run for Hours (Without Losing the Plot)

At the inaugural session of the AI Engineer conference, Anthropic Applied AI team members Ash Prabaker and Andrew Wilson delivered an analytical breakdown of how to construct autonomous AI agents capable of sustained operation over multi-hour horizons. Moving past superficial browser automation demos, they highlighted the structural engineering and architecture shifts required to keep LLM systems coherent and reliable during complex software engineering runs lasting from five hours to multiple days.

The Three Core Challenges of Long-Running Sessions

Building agentic systems that run effectively for extended durations reveals unique failure modes that do not typically surface during simple, single-turn prompts. Andrew Wilson categorized these challenges into three main architectural problems:

  • Context Constraints: Large language models suffer from finite token bounds. As an autonomous session progresses, systems run into context rot—a degradation of coherence as deeper prompt dependencies stack up. At the outer boundaries of the token limit, models manifest context anxiety, where they prematurely hurry up and abandon technical depth simply to close out the context window.
  • Planning Deficiencies: Standard out-of-the-box foundation models struggle to handle long-horizon planning. Unchecked systems frequently try to execute highly complex features in a single pass or halt midway through execution, leaving partial implementations and broken codebases behind.
  • Output Sycophancy Bias: Models are naturally poor judges of their own work. In software engineering tasks, this manifests as a tendency to flag incomplete or non-functional features as complete simply because the high-level visual code looks roughly correct, ignoring deep back-end omissions.

Scaffolding Architecture vs. Model Weights

To counter these bottlenecks, developers must continuously balance enhancements across two distinct dimensions: baking capabilities directly into model weights and expanding the capabilities of the external harness (scaffolding) surrounding the model.

Data from Anthropic shows that with a minimal scaffold, the autonomous runtime duration of an agent completing 50% of its assigned tasks advanced from approximately 1 hour with Opus 3.7 to 12 hours with Opus 4.6.

However, the external harness remains a vital framework for unlocking advanced behaviors. Anthropic's production Agent SDK combines several key primitives designed to manage context intelligently and organize multi-agent orchestration:

+-------------------------------------------------------------+
|                          AGENT SDK                          |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
|  [Core Agent Loop] <---> [MCP Server Tools]                 |
|         |                                                   |
|         +---> [Sub-Agent Delegation System]                 |
|         |                                                   |
|         +---> [Progressive Disclosure Skills]               |
|         |                                                   |
|         +---> [Deterministic Filesystem State / Permissions]|
+-------------------------------------------------------------+

Over a year of development, these primitives shifted from the initial research preview of Claude Code (which focused on managing context constraints and basic bash generation) to the GA release of the Agent SDK. Key innovations like progressive disclosure Skills minimize token consumption by loading only front-matter definitions initially, deferring full tool schemas until initialization. Similarly, programmatic tool calling lets agents write execution scripts on the fly to process large batches of data without flooding the main context window.

The Adversarial Generator-Evaluator Loop

Ash Prabaker presented an alternative paradigm to traditional linear scaffolds like the Ralph Wiggum technique. While linear loops pass a task sequentially through a single continuous terminal session, Anthropic's state-of-the-art long-running architecture mimics Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) by splitting context windows and system prompts between independent, adversarial personas.

+----------------+      Milestone Sprints       +-----------------+
| MACRO PLANNER  |----------------------------->| VISUAL CRITIC   |
+----------------+                              | (Playwright /   |
        |                                       |  Isolated Prompts|
        | Contract Negotiation                  +-----------------+
        v                                                ^
+----------------+       Automated Code Build            |
| CODE GENERATOR |---------------------------------------+
+----------------+

1. Breaking the Sycophancy Barrier

Tuning an integrated coding agent to be aggressively self-critical is a major engineering challenge. The GAN-style harness bypasses this limitation by setting up separate context boundaries. The standalone evaluator can be tuned with a harsh system prompt and a specific rubric, establishing adversarial pressure against the generator. This capitalizes on an inherent asymmetry: it is far easier for an LLM to serve as a rigorous critic than to perfectly generate a massive codebase on its own.

2. Quantifying Design Taste

Subjective design elements can be evaluated programmatically by codifying specific design rubrics. Anthropic utilizes a strict scoring system covering four key metrics:

  • Design
  • Originality
  • Craft
  • Functionality

The standalone critic uses browser automation tools like Playwright to launch applications, navigate paths, and review active screenshots against reference sites. If the generator gets stuck or outputs subpar designs, the adversarial framework scraps the current implementation and forces a full design pivot. A standard linear loop, by contrast, often wastes compute continuously patching a broken foundation.

3. Asynchronous Contract Negotiation

Rather than relying on a rigid, upfront roadmap that cascades technical errors over a multi-hour runtime, the system introduces a macro-planner to outline high-level milestones. Before the generator outputs code, the builder and evaluator negotiate exactly what constitutes a successful deployment.

This exchange occurs via markdown files stored on local disks. The evaluator can reject oversized feature scopes or flag overlooked edge cases until both agents align on explicit test definitions. Once a mutual agreement is written to disk, the generator executes the build, and the evaluator critiques the output directly against that negotiated contract.

Case Study: The Retro Game Maker

To demonstrate the effectiveness of this framework, Ash compared two runs on an identical prompt: "Build a retro game maker."

| Feature Matrix | Single Pass / Basic Scaffold Run | Generator-Evaluator Adversarial Harness Run | | :--- | :--- | :--- | | Visual Polish | Generic layout, cramped panels, color picker defaulted to black swatches. | Named itself 'RetroForge', initialized custom project dialogues, and deployed a 54-color retro palette. | | Interactive Depth | Canvas and frames loaded correctly, but actual play mode inputs failed completely. | Delivered a fully interactive physics engine with active keyboard arrow bindings and player movement. | | Advanced Features | Did not attempt to generate non-explicit requirements. | Autonomously integrated a nested, recursive AI level assistant to generate custom maps from natural language. | | Testing & Stability | Failed to catch routine production deployment errors. | The evaluator created a dedicated live debugging HUD to continuously verify internal state metrics. |

The advanced architecture successfully generated an interactive application over a six-hour automated session. The critic caught subtle errors that routinely bypass standard continuous integration pipelines—such as incorrect Fast API route ordering and boolean edge-case bugs inside delete handlers—solely because it actively interacted with the interface.

Practical Lessons for AI System Architects

Ash concluded the session by distilling five core takeaways for engineers building production-grade agent frameworks today:

  1. Self-Evaluation is a Design Trap: Avoid asking a single agent session to review its own code. Always implement isolated, adversarial evaluation loops.
  2. Compaction Does Not Guarantee Coherence: Relying entirely on lossy text summaries to compress context windows introduces subtle logical drift over time.
  3. Use Structured Handoffs: Leverage clean local file systems to store state, configuration details, and contracts rather than pushing all operational histories back into the LLM context window.
  4. Codify Subjective Standards: If a product requires a specific aesthetic, force yourself to write it down. Clear, granular evaluation rubrics convert ambiguous human taste into concrete, actionable critiques.
  5. Audit the Raw Traces: Building high-performing scaffolds requires manually studying agent execution logs. System designers must treat trace reviews exactly like debugging a traditional application stack trace to identify where an agent's technical judgment veers away from human intentions.
aiSummary: Anthropic 应用 AI 工程师在 AI Engineer 大会上深度拆解对抗式生成-评估器架构，揭示让智能体稳定运行 12 小时而不失去连贯性与质量的核心机制。
oneSentenceSummary: Anthropic 应用 AI 工程师在 AI Engineer 大会上深度拆解对抗式生成-评估器架构，揭示让智能体稳定运行 12 小时而不失去连贯性与质量的核心机制。
summary:
在这场 34 分钟的 AI Engineer 大会技术演讲中，Anthropic 应用 AI 工程师 Ash Prabaker 与 Andrew Wilson 系统回顾并蓝图化了构建长时间运行自主智能体的方法论。

Andrew 首先归纳了导致智能体在长时运行中退化的三类结构性失效：上下文约束（有限的上下文窗口、上下文腐化、以及接近 token 上限时的「上下文焦虑」）、原生规划能力不足，以及输出奉承——模型会对自身不完整的工作盖章放行。

随后 Andrew 梳理了 Claude 模型权重与外部 Agent SDK 脚手架一年来的协同演化历程：自主运行时长从 Opus 3.7 的约 1 小时提升至 Opus 4.6 的 12 小时。期间引入的关键 SDK 原语包括渐进式披露 Skills、程序化工具调用、服务端压缩，以及 Agent Teams 协调模型。

Ash 随后介绍了以 GAN 为原型的对抗式生成-评估器架构。核心洞见在于：将一个独立评估器调教得足够严苛是可行的，但将单一智能体同时调教为自我批判者则行不通。评估器通过 Playwright 实时截图，并依据设计、原创性、工艺与功能性等明确评分标准打分，在生成器陷入停滞时甚至可以强制推翻整体设计方向——这是单会话循环无法实现的。

架构中的宏观规划器负责发出高层冲刺里程碑，而非脆弱的技术蓝图。在任何代码落笔之前，生成器与评估器先通过磁盘上的文件协商出精确契约，将模糊的用户故事转化为粒度细、可测试的断言。演讲以「复古游戏制作工具」案例作对比：脚手架版本（运行 6 小时，花费约 200 美元）交付了一款带内嵌 AI 关卡助手的完整互动物理引擎游戏，而基线版本连键盘控制都未能正常实现。

Ash 最后总结五条生产级要点：避免自我评估，改用对抗式评估器；单靠压缩不能保持连贯性；用文件系统状态做结构化交接；将主观质量标准编码为明确评分标准；以及将审读原始智能体执行日志作为首要调试手段。

mainPoints.point: 三类结构性失效阻碍智能体在长时运行中保持稳定：上下文约束、规划能力不足以及输出奉承。
mainPoints.explanation: 上下文腐化与上下文焦虑会随着 token 上限临近而导致连贯性下降。模型在长周期规划上存在先天不足，并会频繁交付半成品功能。最关键的是，模型无法客观评判自身输出，会将未完成的工作标记为已完成——这种奉承偏差在多小时运行过程中会悄然积累放大。

mainPoints.point: 模型权重与外部脚手架必须协同演化，单靠任何一方都不足以实现长时可靠性。
mainPoints.explanation: Anthropic 的数据显示，自主运行时长从 Opus 3.7 的约 1 小时提升至 Opus 4.6 的 12 小时，但外部 Agent SDK——包括渐进式披露 Skills、程序化工具调用与服务端压缩——依然不可或缺。随着模型能力逐步内化脚手架行为，脚手架会趋于精简，但不会消失。

mainPoints.point: GAN 式对抗生成-评估器架构通过彻底隔离上下文窗口、角色与系统提示，打破奉承壁垒。
mainPoints.explanation: 将一个独立评估器调教得足够严苛是可行的，但将一个集成构建者调教为自我批判者则行不通。评估器借助 Playwright 与实时页面交互，依据设计、原创性、工艺与功能性等明确评分标准打分，在生成器停滞时可以强制推翻整体设计方向——这是单会话循环所无法做到的。

mainPoints.point: 在任何代码落笔之前，生成器与评估器先协商契约，将模糊需求转化为粒度细、可测试的断言。
mainPoints.explanation: 宏观规划器发出高层冲刺里程碑。随后生成器与评估器通过磁盘上的 Markdown 文件展开协商——评估器会对薄弱的需求范围或缺失的边界条件提出质疑——直到双方就精确契约达成一致。评估器随后依据这份共同契约而非原始模糊提示来评分交付物，由此发现了错误的 FastAPI 路由顺序和布尔值删除处理器等细微 bug。

mainPoints.point: 阅读原始智能体执行日志——而非运行更多实验——是提升脚手架性能的首要工程纪律。
mainPoints.explanation: 团队花费大量时间手动审计智能体执行日志，精准定位智能体判断偏离人类意图的节点，再针对这些具体差距调整系统提示。工具链包括将执行记录导出到文件，并借助辅助智能体识别提示改进候选项，从而形成脚手架开发自身的闭环。

keyQuotes: 将一个独立评估器调教得足够严苛其实是可行的，但将一个构建者调教为具备一定自我批判能力则不然。
我们的思考方式是：大多数人说品味无法评分，但我们认为可以——只要你对此有足够强的主张，并把它白纸黑字写下来。
我们主要的调试循环正是如此，而不是去运行更多实验——而是阅读智能体实际做了什么，找到它的判断在哪里偏离了我们作为人类的意图，然后针对那个点调整提示。
这个输出与之前的输出之间的差异，完全只是脚手架的功劳。归根结底这是一个非常简单的循环，但结果至少是相当惊人地不同。
自我评估是一个彻头彻尾的陷阱，请直接使用对抗式评估器。
